manifest.title: End-to-end pipeline for Parameter Golf lane_3: data flows from training data int
manifest.caption: End-to-end pipeline for Parameter Golf lane_3: data flows from training data into a float32 teacher model and a student model that undergoes staged QAT. Show (1) float-only training segment, (2) QAT phase with fake-quant nodes, (3) roundtrip path where student weights/activations are quantized to int8 and then compressed with zlib, and (4) evaluation of post-roundtrip val_bpb under a 16MB artifact cap and ≤1.10× runtime constraint. Annotate where loss terms L_task, KD loss, quantization error loss, and compression-aware losses plug into the training loop.
manifest.label: fig:pipeline_overview_1

manifest.title: Diagram of the compression-teacher distillation setup (Hypothesis 1). Show a flo
manifest.caption: Diagram of the compression-teacher distillation setup (Hypothesis 1). Show a float teacher network producing logits and (optionally) feature maps for distillation, alongside a student network that passes through fake-quant nodes to approximate int8. Highlight selected tensors (e.g., final-layer weights or activations) that are routed through a zlib compress/decompress block during training to compute L_zlib. Illustrate computation of teacher-relative compressibility target alpha and the additional L_comp term, then aggregation into L_total = L_task + lambda_kd * L_KD + lambda_comp * L_comp.
manifest.label: fig:architecture_diagram_2

manifest.title: Flowchart for compression-biased stochastic gating (Hypothesis 2). Steps: (1) pe
manifest.caption: Flowchart for compression-biased stochastic gating (Hypothesis 2). Steps: (1) periodically sample a minibatch, quantize per-channel activations, and pass channel-wise bytes through zlib to get bpb_c; (2) update EMA statistics h_c for each channel/head; (3) for each training step, compute p_drop(c) from h_c relative to the mean compressibility, sample binary gates g_c, and apply them to activations; (4) in inference, all gates fixed to 1 (no gating). Include annotations that this operates only during QAT, does not change the deployed graph, and consumes sparse zlib calls.
manifest.label: fig:method_flowchart_3

manifest.title: Effect of QAT + Roundtrip-Aware Compression on Validation bpb
manifest.caption: Comparison of validation bits-per-byte (val_bpb) between the float-only baseline and the proposed staged QAT with int8+zlib roundtrip alignment under the same gate budget. Lower val_bpb indicates better compression performance, directly testing the hypothesis that roundtrip-aware QAT improves post-roundtrip validation compression quality versus float-only training.
manifest.label: fig:fig_main_results_val_bpb

manifest.title: Runtime Overhead and Quick-Gate Constraint
manifest.caption: Runtime comparison between the float-only baseline and the proposed QAT + roundtrip-aware compression lane, showing absolute runtime (ms) and the runtime ratio relative to baseline. A dashed reference line at 1.10× indicates the quick-gate threshold; bars above this line would violate the runtime constraint, while the quick_gate_passed indicator confirms whether the candidate configuration satisfies the artifact limit of ≤1.10× baseline runtime.
manifest.label: fig:fig_runtime_quick_gate

manifest.title: Illustration of evaluation constraints and metrics. Show a schematic comparing b
manifest.caption: Illustration of evaluation constraints and metrics. Show a schematic comparing baseline float-only + post-hoc quantization vs multiple QAT variants (standard QAT+distillation, codec-guided QAT, compression-biased gating). For each, depict the measurement points for val_bpb after int8+zlib roundtrip, runtime (with a 1.10× quick-gate threshold), and artifact size with a 16MB cap. Emphasize that all methods share the same architecture and int8 scheme and are judged in terms of post-roundtrip behavior.
manifest.label: fig:concept_illustration_4

manifest.title: Change in Validation bpb Relative to Baseline
manifest.caption: Delta in validation bits-per-byte (delta_bpb) for the QAT + roundtrip-aware method relative to the float-only baseline, where negative values correspond to an improvement in compression. This figure isolates the net effect size of the proposed lane on val_bpb, complementing the absolute metrics in the main results figure.
manifest.label: fig:fig_delta_bpb_improvement

markdown_refs:
![Figure 1: End-to-end pipeline for Parameter Golf lane_3: data flows from training data into a float32 teacher model and a student model that undergoes staged QAT. Show (1) float-only training segment, (2) QAT phase with fake-quant nodes, (3) roundtrip path where student weights/activations are quantized to int8 and then compressed with zlib, and (4) evaluation of post-roundtrip val_bpb under a 16MB artifact cap and ≤1.10× runtime constraint. Annotate where loss terms L_task, KD loss, quantization error loss, and compression-aware losses plug into the training loop.](charts/pipeline_overview_1.png)

![Figure 2: Diagram of the compression-teacher distillation setup (Hypothesis 1). Show a float teacher network producing logits and (optionally) feature maps for distillation, alongside a student network that passes through fake-quant nodes to approximate int8. Highlight selected tensors (e.g., final-layer weights or activations) that are routed through a zlib compress/decompress block during training to compute L_zlib. Illustrate computation of teacher-relative compressibility target alpha and the additional L_comp term, then aggregation into L_total = L_task + lambda_kd * L_KD + lambda_comp * L_comp.](charts/architecture_diagram_2.png)

![Figure 3: Flowchart for compression-biased stochastic gating (Hypothesis 2). Steps: (1) periodically sample a minibatch, quantize per-channel activations, and pass channel-wise bytes through zlib to get bpb_c; (2) update EMA statistics h_c for each channel/head; (3) for each training step, compute p_drop(c) from h_c relative to the mean compressibility, sample binary gates g_c, and apply them to activations; (4) in inference, all gates fixed to 1 (no gating). Include annotations that this operates only during QAT, does not change the deployed graph, and consumes sparse zlib calls.](charts/method_flowchart_3.png)

![Figure 4: Comparison of validation bits-per-byte (val_bpb) between the float-only baseline and the proposed staged QAT with int8+zlib roundtrip alignment under the same gate budget. Lower val_bpb indicates better compression performance, directly testing the hypothesis that roundtrip-aware QAT improves post-roundtrip validation compression quality versus float-only training.](charts/fig_main_results_val_bpb.png)

![Figure 5: Runtime comparison between the float-only baseline and the proposed QAT + roundtrip-aware compression lane, showing absolute runtime (ms) and the runtime ratio relative to baseline. A dashed reference line at 1.10× indicates the quick-gate threshold; bars above this line would violate the runtime constraint, while the quick_gate_passed indicator confirms whether the candidate configuration satisfies the artifact limit of ≤1.10× baseline runtime.](charts/fig_runtime_quick_gate.png)

![Figure 6: Illustration of evaluation constraints and metrics. Show a schematic comparing baseline float-only + post-hoc quantization vs multiple QAT variants (standard QAT+distillation, codec-guided QAT, compression-biased gating). For each, depict the measurement points for val_bpb after int8+zlib roundtrip, runtime (with a 1.10× quick-gate threshold), and artifact size with a 16MB cap. Emphasize that all methods share the same architecture and int8 scheme and are judged in terms of post-roundtrip behavior.](charts/concept_illustration_4.png)

![Figure 7: Delta in validation bits-per-byte (delta_bpb) for the QAT + roundtrip-aware method relative to the float-only baseline, where negative values correspond to an improvement in compression. This figure isolates the net effect size of the proposed lane on val_bpb, complementing the absolute metrics in the main results figure.](charts/fig_delta_bpb_improvement.png)

## AVAILABLE FIGURES (embed in the paper)
The following figures were generated from actual experiment data. Reference them in the appropriate paper sections using markdown image syntax: `![Caption](charts/filename.png)`

**Figure 1** (`charts/pipeline_overview_1.png`) — End-to-end pipeline for Parameter Golf lane_3: data flows from training data int
  Caption: End-to-end pipeline for Parameter Golf lane_3: data flows from training data into a float32 teacher model and a student model that undergoes staged QAT. Show (1) float-only training segment, (2) QAT phase with fake-quant nodes, (3) roundtrip path where student weights/activations are quantized to int8 and then compressed with zlib, and (4) evaluation of post-roundtrip val_bpb under a 16MB artifact cap and ≤1.10× runtime constraint. Annotate where loss terms L_task, KD loss, quantization error loss, and compression-aware losses plug into the training loop.
  Place in: **Method** section

**Figure 2** (`charts/architecture_diagram_2.png`) — Diagram of the compression-teacher distillation setup (Hypothesis 1). Show a flo
  Caption: Diagram of the compression-teacher distillation setup (Hypothesis 1). Show a float teacher network producing logits and (optionally) feature maps for distillation, alongside a student network that passes through fake-quant nodes to approximate int8. Highlight selected tensors (e.g., final-layer weights or activations) that are routed through a zlib compress/decompress block during training to compute L_zlib. Illustrate computation of teacher-relative compressibility target alpha and the additional L_comp term, then aggregation into L_total = L_task + lambda_kd * L_KD + lambda_comp * L_comp.
  Place in: **Method** section

**Figure 3** (`charts/method_flowchart_3.png`) — Flowchart for compression-biased stochastic gating (Hypothesis 2). Steps: (1) pe
  Caption: Flowchart for compression-biased stochastic gating (Hypothesis 2). Steps: (1) periodically sample a minibatch, quantize per-channel activations, and pass channel-wise bytes through zlib to get bpb_c; (2) update EMA statistics h_c for each channel/head; (3) for each training step, compute p_drop(c) from h_c relative to the mean compressibility, sample binary gates g_c, and apply them to activations; (4) in inference, all gates fixed to 1 (no gating). Include annotations that this operates only during QAT, does not change the deployed graph, and consumes sparse zlib calls.
  Place in: **Method** section

**Figure 4** (`charts/fig_main_results_val_bpb.png`) — Effect of QAT + Roundtrip-Aware Compression on Validation bpb
  Caption: Comparison of validation bits-per-byte (val_bpb) between the float-only baseline and the proposed staged QAT with int8+zlib roundtrip alignment under the same gate budget. Lower val_bpb indicates better compression performance, directly testing the hypothesis that roundtrip-aware QAT improves post-roundtrip validation compression quality versus float-only training.
  Place in: **Results** section

**Figure 5** (`charts/fig_runtime_quick_gate.png`) — Runtime Overhead and Quick-Gate Constraint
  Caption: Runtime comparison between the float-only baseline and the proposed QAT + roundtrip-aware compression lane, showing absolute runtime (ms) and the runtime ratio relative to baseline. A dashed reference line at 1.10× indicates the quick-gate threshold; bars above this line would violate the runtime constraint, while the quick_gate_passed indicator confirms whether the candidate configuration satisfies the artifact limit of ≤1.10× baseline runtime.
  Place in: **Results** section

**Figure 6** (`charts/concept_illustration_4.png`) — Illustration of evaluation constraints and metrics. Show a schematic comparing b
  Caption: Illustration of evaluation constraints and metrics. Show a schematic comparing baseline float-only + post-hoc quantization vs multiple QAT variants (standard QAT+distillation, codec-guided QAT, compression-biased gating). For each, depict the measurement points for val_bpb after int8+zlib roundtrip, runtime (with a 1.10× quick-gate threshold), and artifact size with a 16MB cap. Emphasize that all methods share the same architecture and int8 scheme and are judged in terms of post-roundtrip behavior.
  Place in: **Results** section

**Figure 7** (`charts/fig_delta_bpb_improvement.png`) — Change in Validation bpb Relative to Baseline
  Caption: Delta in validation bits-per-byte (delta_bpb) for the QAT + roundtrip-aware method relative to the float-only baseline, where negative values correspond to an improvement in compression. This figure isolates the net effect size of the proposed lane on val_bpb, complementing the absolute metrics in the main results figure.
  Place in: **Analysis** section
